title: Smoky Chewy Kenyan Lamb Grill
description: A slowgrilled lamb dish with a sweet smoked paprika rub that delivers smoky flavor and tender chew, perfect for a solo Kenyan dinner.
instructions: In a small bowl, combine smoked paprika, sweet paprika, brown sugar, cumin, coriander, salt, and black pepper to form a dry rub.
Pat the lamb shoulder dry with paper towels, then drizzle with olive oil and sprinkle the rub evenly all over the meat, pressing it into the surface.
Let the seasoned lamb rest covered at room temperature for 20 minutes to absorb flavors.
Preheat a charcoal or gas grill to medium‑high heat (about 200‑220 °C). Sear the lamb on all sides until a dark crust forms, about 3‑4 minutes per side.
Move the lamb to a cooler part of the grill or reduce heat to medium (about 160 °C). Cover and cook for 1 ½ to 2 hours, turning occasionally, until the internal temperature reaches 63 °C (145 °F).
Using a meat thermometer, check the center of the lamb; if it hasn’t reached 63 °C, continue cooking, checking every 10 minutes.
When done, remove the lamb from the grill and tent loosely with foil. Let it rest for 10 minutes before slicing; this allows juices to redistribute and keeps the meat chewy.
Slice the lamb thinly across the grain, serve immediately with a side of roasted sweet potatoes or a simple salad.
Enjoy the smoky, tender lamb as a hearty solo dinner.
